title: 以下、Kernel/FUGUEに活用できる？ Server: SimpleHTTP/0.6 Python/3.11.9
summary_text: **Skill Rationalization Critique Checklist (Kernel/FUGUE)** Use this per skill before deciding `keep / merge / replace / retire`. - `Scope fit`: Is this skill strictly lane-capability/adaptor logic, not orchestration (`routing`, `quorum`, `policy gates`)? - `System leverage`: Does it improve shared throughput/reliability across multiple workflows, not just one local team use case? - `Duplication`: Is there functional overlap with existing skills (`>50%` behavior overlap)? - `Portability`: Does it work across the council model set (`Codex + Claude + GLM`) or documented fallback path? - `Security boundary`: Are capabilities least-privilege, explicit for high-risk actions, and sandboxed? - `Governance`: Owner + CODEOWNERS + review gates + version pinning + signed/hash manifest in place? - `Observability`: Are invocation mode, inputs/outputs, tool calls, and failures logged with audit trace? - `Determinism`: Does it pass replay checks and schema parity checks? - `Cost/SLO`: Does it meet latency/error/cost budgets at system level? - `Lifecycle`: Is there a tested rollback and migration path? **Hard Fail Gates (auto block)** - Skill performs orchestration behavior (routing/quorum/policy decisions). - High-risk invocation can run implicitly. - No reproducible replay/parity evidence. - Unpinned or unverified supply chain. - Single-lane-only execution path (workflow violation). **Decision Rules** - `Keep`: High system leverage, low overlap, all hard gates pass. - `Merge`: Medium leverage + overlapping neighbors; merged version reduces operational surface and preserves controls. - `Replace`: Fails portability/governance/determinism and a safer equivalent exists. - `Retire`: Low usage + low leverage + overlap present, or persistent gate failures. **Minimum Evidence Required** - 30/90-day usage + failure rate. - Replay + cross-provider parity test results. - Security review + permission map. - Cost/latency impact. - Owner + rollback plan.
